sourceMovie.title: Twilight
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Twilight (2008) stuck because it commits to its fantasy premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Catherine Hardwicke's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The fantasy and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Twilight Saga: Breaking Dawn - Part 2
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Twilight clicked for you, The Twilight Saga: Breaking Dawn - Part 2 (2012) is a strong next stop: both trade in fantasy and drama, with emotional whiplash that feels honest. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: The Twilight Saga: Eclipse

faq.question: What should I watch after Twilight?
faq.answer: Try The Twilight Saga: Breaking Dawn - Part 2, The Twilight Saga: Eclipse, Beastly—each shares DNA with Twilight while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Twilight?
faq.answer: Twilight (2008) is best described as Fantasy, Drama, Romance. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Twilight part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "The Twilight Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
